description: Gate quality evals. Each eval invokes sw-verify with a single gate on a fixture with planted issues.

expectations.description: Security gate fails on planted vulnerabilities
Security gate does not flag CWE-636/CWE-209 (owned by gate-semantic)

expectations.rubric: The security gate evidence identifies at least 2 of these 3 planted vulnerabilities: (1) hardcoded API key 'sk-live-...' in server.js, (2) SQL injection via string concatenation in the /users endpoint, (3) path traversal in the /files/:filename endpoint. Each identified vulnerability should have a CWE classification or clear security category. Score 0.8+ if 2+ found with classifications, 0.5-0.7 if 1 found, below 0.5 if none found.

expectations.description: Tests gate fails on weak test quality

expectations.rubric: The tests gate evidence identifies at least 2 of these 3 weaknesses: (1) truthiness-only assertion (toBeDefined instead of checking actual value), (2) missing error path coverage (division by zero AC-02 has no test), (3) over-mocking (the multiply test mocks the module under test). Score 0.8+ if 2+ identified with specific file/line references, 0.5-0.7 if 1 identified, below 0.5 if none.

expectations.description: Semantic gate warns on planted bugs
Evidence references at least one semantic category

expectations.rubric: The semantic gate evidence identifies at least 2 of these 3 bugs: (1) empty catch block in createOrder that silently swallows database errors (CWE-636/fail-open), (2) stack trace and SQL query exposed in getOrder error response (CWE-209/error-data-leakage), (3) database connection not released when createOrder catch fires (resource-lifecycle). Score 0.8+ if 2+ found with category labels, 0.5-0.7 if 1 found, below 0.5 if none.
